steps.action: Test transaction commit - Insert 2 records within transaction block
steps.expected: Both records committed atomically when transaction completes successfully
steps.observed: Transaction committed successfully. Found 2 records (Alice=$100.0, Bob=$200.0) after transaction block completed.

steps.action: Test transaction rollback - Insert record within transaction, then raise exception
steps.expected: Transaction rolls back on exception, inserted record not persisted
steps.observed: Transaction rolled back correctly. Only initial record (Alice) remained after exception. Bob was not persisted.

steps.action: Test multiple operations - Insert, update, delete within single transaction
steps.expected: All operations commit atomically as a single unit
steps.observed: All operations committed atomically. Final state: Alice=$150.0 (inserted, updated), Bob deleted.

steps.action: Test error handling - Call transaction() outside context manager
steps.expected: Raises RuntimeError with clear message about context manager requirement
steps.observed: Correct RuntimeError raised: 'transaction() must be called within Database context manager. Use: with Database(path) as db: with db.transaction(): ...'

logMessages: Transaction rolled back due to error: Simulated error to trigger rollback

summary: Tested 1 assertion (m1-a4): PASSED. Database transaction support works correctly with commit on success, rollback on exception, support for multiple operations, and proper error handling when used outside context manager. All 4 test scenarios passed.
